text.title: Container Security Scanning and Image Signing Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: CI green for months; one morning every PR fails on the same CVE — a Trivy DB update changed the severity classification.
antipattern.root-cause: Action pinned with `@master`; rule-pack updated upstream; previously-passing build now fails without any code change.
antipattern.fix: Pin to a release tag (e.g. `@0.50.0`) or SHA. Bump on a schedule with a known changelog.

antipattern.symptom: Dependency-Track ingests CycloneDX but SIEM expects SPDX; half the data missing on either side.
antipattern.root-cause: Two parallel jobs author SBOMs in different formats because nobody wrote a standard.
antipattern.fix: Pick one format org-wide. Add a CI check that rejects PRs introducing the other format.

antipattern.symptom: Cosign private key committed to repo (even encrypted); leaked when repo went public for one day.
antipattern.root-cause: Author followed an old tutorial that pre-dated keyless OIDC.
antipattern.fix: Rotate the key (revoke via Fulcio if used). Switch to keyless OIDC. Add a pre-commit hook that blocks `cosign.key` filenames.

antipattern.symptom: Scan reports 12 CRITICAL CVEs; CI passes; image pushed; production exposed.
antipattern.root-cause: Scanner step missing `exit-code: '1'` — author copied a 'just report' example.
antipattern.fix: Set `exit-code: '1'` AND `severity: CRITICAL,HIGH`. Verify the push step has `needs: scan` so it only runs on scan success.
